title: Source Trial: Verify a Web-Platform Claim
summary: Judge conflicting technical claims through a precise question, current primary sources, scoped official guidance, and a reduced browser test.
objectives: Evaluate technical sources for authority, currency, scope, and relevance.
Connect a documented claim to reproducible browser evidence and recorded uncertainty.

steps.title: Rank the evidence · conflicting-guidance hearing
steps.instruction: In the conflicting-guidance hearing, which combination provides the strongest basis for a platform claim?
steps.why: Technical guidance is safer when authority, currency, scope, and browser evidence agree. Working through rank the evidence narrows the conflicting-guidance hearing before the result is shown.

content.title: Rank the evidence — conflicting-guidance hearing
content.text: Commit to the claim that explains the conflicting-guidance hearing evidence without assigning work to the wrong layer.

hints: During conflicting-guidance hearing predict, identify which source verification boundary “Rank the evidence” asks about.
Compare every conflicting-guidance hearing option with the named “Rank the evidence” evidence during predict.
For conflicting-guidance hearing predict, reject claims that skip a required layer or invent unavailable evidence for “Rank the evidence.”

options.text: The first search snippet plus the number of advertisements on its page.
A current primary requirement, scoped official explanation, and reproducible browser test.
An undated forum answer repeated by several copied blog posts.

steps.title: Interpret a reload · conflicting-guidance hearing
steps.instruction: In the conflicting-guidance hearing, why did the edited heading revert?
steps.why: A deliberate panel choice turns browser inspection into evidence instead of random experimentation. Working through interpret a reload narrows the conflicting-guidance hearing while you inspect the artifact.

content.title: Interpret a reload — conflicting-guidance hearing
content.text: Commit to the claim that explains the conflicting-guidance hearing evidence without assigning work to the wrong layer.

hints: During conflicting-guidance hearing inspect, identify which devtools evidence boundary “Interpret a reload” asks about.
Compare every conflicting-guidance hearing option with the named “Interpret a reload” evidence during inspect.
For conflicting-guidance hearing inspect, reject claims that skip a required layer or invent unavailable evidence for “Interpret a reload.”

options.text: The monitor cached the old pixels inside the display cable.
The Elements edit changed the current DOM experiment, not the saved HTML file.
HTML headings are forbidden from being saved after DevTools opens.

stimulus.title: Temporary edit versus saved file · conflicting-guidance hearing
stimulus.caption: Evidence collected before and after reloading the page. Case: conflicting-guidance hearing; focus: interpret a reload.

steps.title: Choose structural evidence · conflicting-guidance hearing
steps.instruction: In the conflicting-guidance hearing, which artifact best answers what nodes currently exist?
steps.why: Source, the browser-built DOM, layout, and pixels are related stages rather than interchangeable evidence. Working through choose structural evidence narrows the conflicting-guidance hearing while you defend the selected claim.

content.title: Choose structural evidence — conflicting-guidance hearing
content.text: Commit to the claim that explains the conflicting-guidance hearing evidence without assigning work to the wrong layer.

hints: During conflicting-guidance hearing answer, identify which browser pipeline boundary “Choose structural evidence” asks about.
Compare every conflicting-guidance hearing option with the named “Choose structural evidence” evidence during answer.
For conflicting-guidance hearing answer, reject claims that skip a required layer or invent unavailable evidence for “Choose structural evidence.”

options.text: A screenshot of the text color.
The inspected DOM tree.
The saved filename without its source contents.

steps.title: Record uncertainty · conflicting-guidance hearing
steps.instruction: In the conflicting-guidance hearing, two current sources disagree and the test is inconclusive. What is the professional response?
steps.why: Technical guidance is safer when authority, currency, scope, and browser evidence agree. Working through record uncertainty narrows the conflicting-guidance hearing as you connect the explanation to evidence.

content.text: A useful technical question names the exact behavior and environment. Primary specifications define the platform; official documentation explains it; compatibility data narrows support; and a reduced browser test verifies the local claim. Search rank, confident tone, and copied snippets are not authority. Apply this distinction during the conflicting-guidance hearing read task.

hints: During conflicting-guidance hearing read, identify which source verification boundary “Record uncertainty” asks about.
Compare every conflicting-guidance hearing option with the named “Record uncertainty” evidence during read.
For conflicting-guidance hearing read, reject claims that skip a required layer or invent unavailable evidence for “Record uncertainty.”

options.text: Choose the shortest source and delete evidence of the disagreement.
Document the conflict, environment, and uncertainty; avoid presenting the decision as settled fact.
Present the preferred answer as universal because uncertainty reduces confidence.

steps.title: Rebuild devtools evidence · conflicting-guidance hearing
steps.instruction: Within the conflicting-guidance hearing, reconstruct the devtools evidence in causal order.
steps.why: A deliberate panel choice turns browser inspection into evidence instead of random experimentation. Ordering the conflicting-guidance hearing exposes its first unsupported causal handoff.

content.text: Do not order by vocabulary or visual position. Test whether each event creates the conditions required by the next event in the conflicting-guidance hearing.

hints: In the conflicting-guidance hearing sequence, locate the first event whose required input is not ready.
Separate conflicting-guidance hearing devtools evidence events from later visible outcomes.
Rebuild the conflicting-guidance hearing sequence one causal handoff at a time.

options.text: Reproduce the defect and state the expected behavior
Choose the panel that exposes the responsible layer
Run the smallest temporary experiment that tests the hypothesis
Repair saved source, reload, and verify the evidence again

steps.title: Separate source and DOM · conflicting-guidance hearing
steps.instruction: In the conflicting-guidance hearing, which statement accounts for the recovered tree?
steps.why: Source, the browser-built DOM, layout, and pixels are related stages rather than interchangeable evidence. Working through separate source and dom narrows the conflicting-guidance hearing while you inspect the artifact.

content.title: Separate source and DOM — conflicting-guidance hearing
content.text: Commit to the claim that explains the conflicting-guidance hearing evidence without assigning work to the wrong layer.

hints: During conflicting-guidance hearing inspect, identify which browser pipeline boundary “Separate source and DOM” asks about.
Compare every conflicting-guidance hearing option with the named “Separate source and DOM” evidence during inspect.
For conflicting-guidance hearing inspect, reject claims that skip a required layer or invent unavailable evidence for “Separate source and DOM.”

options.text: Source and DOM must remain identical character for character after parsing.
Source is parser input; the DOM is the browser’s current constructed representation and may include recovery.
The DOM is a screenshot made from the final pixels.

stimulus.title: Recovered document tree · conflicting-guidance hearing
stimulus.caption: The browser-built structure after parsing malformed source. Case: conflicting-guidance hearing; focus: separate source and dom.

lines.label: parser
lines.text: end tags inferred before document end

steps.title: Check source scope · conflicting-guidance hearing
steps.instruction: In the conflicting-guidance hearing, before applying the standard text, what must be confirmed?
steps.why: Technical guidance is safer when authority, currency, scope, and browser evidence agree. Working through check source scope narrows the conflicting-guidance hearing while you defend the selected claim.

content.title: Check source scope — conflicting-guidance hearing
content.text: Commit to the claim that explains the conflicting-guidance hearing evidence without assigning work to the wrong layer.

hints: During conflicting-guidance hearing answer, identify which source verification boundary “Check source scope” asks about.
Compare every conflicting-guidance hearing option with the named “Check source scope” evidence during answer.
For conflicting-guidance hearing answer, reject claims that skip a required layer or invent unavailable evidence for “Check source scope.”

options.text: That the page uses the same brand colors as the project.
That the cited section addresses the same feature, conditions, and current version under investigation.
That the search engine placed it above all official documentation.

steps.title: Write the evidence note · conflicting-guidance hearing
steps.instruction: Write a concise case note for the conflicting-guidance hearing. Explain the mechanism, cite observed evidence, and reject one plausible alternative.
steps.why: Explaining the conflicting-guidance hearing from memory exposes gaps that selected answers can hide. Cite the mechanism and its evidence.

content.title: Transfer brief
content.text: A teammate must be able to reproduce your conclusion without seeing the earlier answer choices. Name evidence and limits, not only the final verdict.

hints: Start the conflicting-guidance hearing case note with the earliest observed event.
Use these conflicting-guidance hearing technical anchors where they fit: primary source, version, browser evidence, Elements, Network.
Close the conflicting-guidance hearing note by naming one alternative and the evidence that rules it out.

checks.description: The predict claim correctly handles rank the evidence in the conflicting-guidance hearing.
checks.failureMessage: During conflicting-guidance hearing predict, recheck the responsible layer and available rank the evidence evidence.

checks.description: The inspect claim correctly handles interpret a reload in the conflicting-guidance hearing.
checks.failureMessage: During conflicting-guidance hearing inspect, recheck the responsible layer and available interpret a reload evidence.

checks.failureMessage: During conflicting-guidance hearing answer, recheck the responsible layer and available choose structural evidence evidence.

checks.description: The read claim correctly handles record uncertainty in the conflicting-guidance hearing.
checks.failureMessage: During conflicting-guidance hearing read, recheck the responsible layer and available record uncertainty evidence.

checks.description: The devtools evidence follows a defensible causal order.
checks.failureMessage: Find the first event whose required input has not happened yet in the devtools evidence.

checks.description: The inspect claim correctly handles separate source and dom in the conflicting-guidance hearing.
checks.failureMessage: During conflicting-guidance hearing inspect, recheck the responsible layer and available separate source and dom evidence.

checks.failureMessage: During conflicting-guidance hearing answer, recheck the responsible layer and available check source scope evidence.

checks.description: The case note explains the model with specific technical evidence.
checks.failureMessage: Expand the note to at least 160 characters and connect the required technical terms to evidence.
